title: Database Internals
subtitle: A Deep Dive into How Distributed Data Systems Work
author: Alex Petrov
themes: Storage engine architecture and data organization on disk
B-Tree and LSM-Tree internals and trade-offs
Failure handling and fault tolerance in distributed databases
awards: Highly rated reference for database engineers
Selected as O'Reilly essential reading for distributed systems
Recommended by CMU database group reading list
excerpt: Database Internals takes you under the hood of modern databases and storage engines, revealing how they organize data on disk, handle concurrent access, and coordinate across machines. Essential reading for engineers who want to understand how databases really work.
